title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Crickenshaw leads Lucy to a large black oak piece of furniture from Lichfield, revealing a secret moneybox containing gold sovereigns. He claims that no one, including Emma, is aware of this collection, and he shows it to Lucy to prove he is not weak. He offers her advice on waiting to control her future before Lucy leaves the room, feeling uncertain about the encounter, after being called away by Emma.

At Scotland Yard, Inspector Cladock discusses the murder investigation via telephone with a French officer. The officer notes the difficulty of identifying strangled women and mentions that many travelers go unnoticed. Emma Crikesopp arrives at the office to report information regarding the murder at Latherford Manor.

Emma presents a faded letter from her late brother, Edmund, which mentions his intention to marry a French woman named Martine. Emma explains that she could find no military records of a marriage and had assumed the union never happened. However, a month prior, Emma received a letter in French script from a woman named Martina Crickenshaw, who claimed to be Edmund's wife and the mother of his son. Martina stated she would be in Britain the following week to visit. Following her brother Harold's advice to be cautious, Emma invited the woman to Latherford. Shortly after, Emma received a telegram from someone named "Matilda" stating she had to return to France unexpectedly, and there has been no contact since.
